trivia_bullets.text: Throne of Eldraine (ELD, October 2019) is a dual-register set: Arthurian Court and Grimm-fairy-tale Wilds. Insatiable Appetite occupies the Wilds layer — the giant who consumes everything a smallfolk community has built — sharing the Jack-and-the-Beanstalk register with Beanstalk Giant and Giant Killer without being one of the named fairy-tale cards.
sources: Wizards: 'More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine,' 2019-10-21

trivia_bullets.text: "He ate them out of house and home and shed and barn and flock and herd." A progressive exhaustion catalog extending the real English idiom 'ate out of house and home' through every layer of agrarian infrastructure. No speaker named; functions as folk-tale narration.

The Food mechanic originates in ELD after Wizards designers noticed a large fraction of their fairy-tale trope list involved food (Hansel and Gretel's breadcrumbs, Little Red Riding Hood's basket, Snow White's poisoned apple, Jack's magic beans). Insatiable Appetite's bonus pump (+5/+5 vs +3/+3) for sacrificing a Food mechanically expresses the theme: the giant grows stronger when fed.

First and only printing (no reprints as of Scryfall oracle search). As a {1}{G} instant that scales with Food sacrifice, it saw play in ELD Limited on the Food-generation axis. Legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, Pauper, and Commander.
Artist Nils Hamm is a German fantasy illustrator with an MTG career stretching back to Zendikar (2009). No dedicated Hipsters of the Coast feature was found for this card.

related_cards.name: Beanstalk Giant
related_cards.note: The named Jack-and-the-Beanstalk giant in the same set; shares the giant-eating-everything flavor register

related_cards.name: Giant Killer
related_cards.note: The Jack-figure in ELD's Jack-and-Beanstalk reference cycle; smallfolk counterpoint to this card's giant perspective

related_cards.name: Gilded Goose
related_cards.note: Golden-goose fairy-tale Food generator; part of the same Food-token design ecosystem this card feeds on

related_cards.name: Feasting Troll King
related_cards.note: Apex giant feaster of the set; same flavor archetype at rare
